articles.headline: PM Inaugurates National Multimodal Logistics Hub, Dedicates Key EDFC Section
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister launched the National Multimodal Logistics Hub (NMLH) in a major push for integrated logistics infrastructure.
• A significant section of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) was also dedicated to the nation, enhancing freight movement efficiency.
• The NMLH aims to streamline supply chains, reduce logistics costs, and improve India's global competitiveness.
• Part of the 'PM Gati Shakti' National Master Plan, these projects are crucial for achieving the 'Viksit Bharat' vision by 2047.
• Focus on integrating various modes of transport – road, rail, air, and waterways – to create a seamless logistics ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: National Multimodal Logistics Hub (NMLH) aims to reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks.
Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) connects Punjab to West Bengal.
PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan is an integrated approach for infrastructure development.
The projects are aligned with the vision of 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of integrated multimodal logistics in boosting India's economic competitiveness and reducing carbon footprint.
Analyze how projects like EDFC and NMLH contribute to the 'PM Gati Shakti' vision and regional development.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in achieving a world-class logistics infrastructure in India.
Evaluate the impact of reduced logistics costs on manufacturing, agriculture, and exports.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister inaugurating a large-scale logistics facility with freight trains in the background.
articles.trivia: India's logistics cost as a percentage of GDP is significantly higher than global averages, making efficient infrastructure crucial.

articles.headline: Class 2 Student Killed in Hathras 'Tantric Sacrificial Rite'; Calls for Anti-Superstition Law Strengthen
articles.explainer:
• A Class 2 student was tragically killed in Hathras district, Uttar Pradesh, reportedly as part of a 'tantric sacrificial rite'.
• The alleged perpetrators are the owners of the school where the child studied, who performed the act for the 'prosperity' of their institute.
• The incident has sparked outrage across the nation, bringing to fore the persistent issue of superstitious practices and black magic.
• Civil society organizations and legal experts are renewing calls for a comprehensive national anti-superstition law.
• Police have initiated an investigation and arrested the accused, promising swift justice in the heinous crime.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
prelimsPoints: Hathras district is in Uttar Pradesh.
The incident highlights the prevalence of superstitious beliefs in some parts of India.
Several states like Maharashtra and Karnataka have anti-superstition laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors that perpetuate superstitious beliefs and practices in modern India.
Analyze the need for a national anti-superstition law, considering constitutional provisions and fundamental rights.
Examine the role of education, awareness campaigns, and community engagement in eradicating social evils like human sacrifice.
Evaluate the challenges faced by law enforcement in tackling crimes motivated by superstitious beliefs.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of a police investigation scene in a rural area, symbolizing law enforcement action.
articles.trivia: Maharashtra was the first Indian state to enact an Anti-Superstition and Black Magic Act in 2013.

articles.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Summit: Leaders Vow to Bolster Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) Summit in New Delhi.
• Leaders from member states emphasized enhancing regional connectivity, trade, and economic cooperation.
• Key agreements were discussed on cross-border energy grids, digital connectivity, and multimodal transport corridors.
• The summit reiterated commitment to collective efforts in disaster management, climate change, and counter-terrorism.
• BIMSTEC aims to strengthen ties among countries in the Bay of Bengal region, bridging South and Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
keyTerms: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC members include Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.
It was established in 1997.
BIMSTEC focuses on 14 priority sectors for cooperation.
The Bay of Bengal region is strategically important for trade routes and energy security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of BIMSTEC for India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
Discuss the potential of enhanced regional connectivity through BIMSTEC to foster economic growth and stability in South and Southeast Asia.
Examine the challenges faced by BIMSTEC in achieving deeper integration and how they can be addressed.
Evaluate BIMSTEC's role in India's broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of BIMSTEC member countries posing for a group photograph at the summit.
articles.trivia: BIMSTEC accounts for around 22% of the world's population and a combined GDP of over $3.8 trillion.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Critical Engine for Gaganyaan Mission, Paving Way for Human Spaceflight
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful long-duration hot test of a critical engine for the Gaganyaan mission.
• This test is a significant milestone in India's human spaceflight program, validating key propulsion systems.
• The engine, part of the service module propulsion system, is designed for orbital maneuvering and de-orbiting the crew module.
• ISRO is progressing steadily towards the uncrewed 'abort mission' tests and subsequent crewed flights.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts into space on an indigenous rocket by 2025.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The mission aims to send astronauts to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
The LVM3 rocket (formerly GSLV Mk-III) will be used for Gaganyaan.
ISRO is based in Bengaluru.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and socio-economic implications of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan).
Analyze the indigenous technological capabilities developed by ISRO for missions like Gaganyaan and their spin-off benefits.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the global space race, particularly in human spaceflight.
Evaluate the role of such ambitious missions in inspiring scientific temperament and innovation among youth.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of a rocket engine test firing, with ISRO logo.
articles.trivia: ISRO has achieved several firsts, including being the fourth space agency to successfully reach Mars orbit.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Key Provisions in Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of significant provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment emphasized the state's legitimate interest in regulating data processing while safeguarding individual privacy.
• Petitions had challenged certain clauses regarding data fiduciaries' obligations and the process of grievance redressal.
• The Court affirmed the 'right to be forgotten' and 'right to correction and erasure' as integral aspects of data protection.
• The ruling provides clarity on the implementation of the new data protection regime, impacting tech companies and citizens alike.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to govern the processing of digital personal data.
The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment (2017) recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
Data Fiduciary refers to any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
Data Principal is the individual to whom the personal data relates.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act, 2023, between individual privacy rights and the state's legitimate interests in data processing.
Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital governance, e-commerce, and the tech industry in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in protecting citizens' data in an increasingly digital world, considering global best practices.
Examine the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, particularly concerning cross-border data flows and enforcement mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing judicial proceedings and law.
articles.trivia: The DPDP Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, which was withdrawn.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Management and Growth Support
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects the RBI's continued focus on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The MPC noted a moderation in core inflation but highlighted risks from volatile food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• Projections for GDP growth for FY25 were maintained, indicating cautious optimism about economic resilience.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to closely monitor evolving economic conditions and take necessary policy actions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate is 6.5%.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members: three from RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the impact of the unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and achieving its stated objectives.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: RBI headquarters building in Mumbai, symbolizing financial policy.
articles.trivia: The repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Unveils National Action Plan for Climate Resilience in Coastal Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a comprehensive National Action Plan for Climate Resilience in Coastal Areas.
• The plan focuses on protecting vulnerable coastal ecosystems, communities, and infrastructure from the impacts of climate change.
• Key strategies include mangrove restoration, coral reef protection, sustainable fisheries, and early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• It integrates traditional ecological knowledge with modern scientific approaches for effective adaptation and mitigation measures.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments under the Paris Agreement and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate Change.
keyTerms: National Action Plan for Climate Resilience
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km.
Mangroves act as natural barriers against storm surges and tsunamis.
Coral reefs are vital marine ecosystems supporting biodiversity.
The plan links to India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
mainsPoints: Discuss the major threats posed by climate change to India's coastal regions and the vulnerable communities residing there.
Analyze the key components of the National Action Plan for Climate Resilience in Coastal Areas and its potential effectiveness.
Examine the role of international cooperation and local community participation in implementing coastal resilience strategies.
Evaluate the economic and ecological benefits of investing in natural infrastructure like mangroves and coral reefs for coastal protection.
articles.imageDescription: A serene coastal landscape featuring mangrove forests protecting the shoreline.
articles.trivia: About 14% of India's population lives in coastal districts, highly vulnerable to climate impacts.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Nechiphu Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh, Enhancing Border Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of the strategic Nechiphu Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh.
• This all-weather tunnel will provide crucial connectivity to Tawang and other forward areas, bypassing challenging terrains.
• The tunnel is part of the larger 'Project Vartak' aimed at improving infrastructure along the India-China border.
• It significantly reduces travel time and ensures uninterrupted movement of troops and supplies, especially during adverse weather conditions.
• The project underscores India's commitment to bolstering its border infrastructure and enhancing national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
prelimsPoints: Nechiphu Tunnel is located in Arunachal Pradesh.
BRO (Border Roads Organisation) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas.
Project Vartak is a BRO project in the Northeast region.
Tawang is a strategically important town in Arunachal Pradesh.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing robust infrastructure along India's northern borders, particularly in Arunachal Pradesh.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing national security and economic development in challenging terrains.
Examine the geopolitical implications of improved border connectivity in the context of India's relations with neighboring countries.
Evaluate the challenges faced in infrastructure development in mountainous border regions and how they are being addressed.
articles.imageDescription: Entrance of a newly constructed tunnel in a mountainous region, with BRO signage.
articles.trivia: The BRO was formed in 1960 and plays a vital role in maintaining the operational preparedness of the armed forces.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Amendments to Election Laws for Enhanced Transparency in Campaign Finance
articles.explainer:
• Parliament has successfully passed significant amendments to election laws, aiming to bring greater transparency to campaign finance.
• The amendments introduce stricter disclosure norms for political donations, including details of donors and beneficiaries.
• Provisions for digitizing election expenditure reports and real-time monitoring by the Election Commission of India (ECI) were also included.
• The move comes amidst long-standing demands for electoral reforms to curb the influence of black money in elections.
• Opposition parties, while supporting the intent, raised concerns about potential loopholes and the need for comprehensive reform.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: The Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs elections in India.
The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Electoral bonds were introduced to promote transparency in political funding, though their anonymity aspect was controversial.
Article 324 of the Constitution deals with the Election Commission.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of electoral finance regulations in India and the persistent challenges in ensuring transparency.
Discuss the significance of the recent amendments to election laws in strengthening democratic processes and curbing corruption.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India in advocating for and implementing electoral reforms.
Examine the debate around political funding mechanisms, including electoral bonds, and suggest further reforms for greater accountability.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a hand inserting a vote, symbolizing electoral process.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in India were held in 1951-52.

articles.headline: India and France Ink MoU for Joint Research in Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence
articles.explainer:
• India and France signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for enhanced cooperation in cutting-edge technologies like Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence (AI).
• The agreement aims to foster joint research projects, talent exchange, and development of shared technological platforms.
• This collaboration is part of the strategic partnership between the two nations, focusing on emerging and critical technologies.
• It will facilitate the exchange of scientific expertise and resources to accelerate innovation in both countries.
• The partnership seeks to leverage the strengths of both nations in building future-ready technological capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a strategic partnership established in 1998.
Quantum computing harnesses quantum-mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement.
Artificial Intelligence (AI) involves developing machines that can perform human-like cognitive functions.
India has a National Mission on Quantum Technologies and Applications (NMQTA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's strategic partnerships, such as with France, in advancing its technological capabilities in emerging fields like Quantum Computing and AI.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of international collaboration in developing critical and emerging technologies.
Examine how advancements in Quantum Computing and AI can impact national security, economic growth, and societal development.
Evaluate India's policy framework for promoting research and innovation in AI and Quantum Technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French flags displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: France is a key partner for India in defense, nuclear energy, and space cooperation.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana 2.0' to Boost Organic and Sustainable Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched 'Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) 2.0', an enhanced scheme to promote organic farming and sustainable agricultural practices.
• The updated scheme focuses on cluster-based organic farming, farmer participatory guarantee systems, and value chain development.
• It aims to reduce reliance on chemical fertilizers and pesticides, improve soil health, and enhance farmers' income through premium organic produce.
• Special provisions were made for promoting organic farming in North-Eastern states and tribal areas.
• The scheme aligns with national goals of food security, environmental sustainability, and rural development.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: PKVY is a component of the National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA).
PGS (Participatory Guarantee System) is a quality assurance initiative for organic products.
Sikkim is India's first fully organic state.
The scheme aims to promote chemical-free farming.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind promoting organic and sustainable agriculture in India, considering environmental, economic, and health perspectives.
Analyze the key features and improvements of 'Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana 2.0' in fostering organic farming practices.
Examine the challenges faced by organic farmers in India, including certification, market access, and yield management.
Evaluate the potential of organic farming to enhance farmers' income and contribute to India's food security goals.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush green organic field, symbolizing sustainable practices.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries in terms of organic agricultural land and organic producers.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Report Highlights Significant Progress in Maternal and Child Health Indicators
articles.explainer:
• The latest report from the National Health Mission (NHM) highlighted substantial improvements in key maternal and child health (MCH) indicators across India.
• Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR), Infant Mortality Rate (IMR), and Under-5 Mortality Rate (U5MR) have shown a consistent downward trend.
• The report attributes this success to enhanced institutional deliveries, increased access to antenatal and postnatal care, and immunization coverage.
• Focus on Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram (JSSK) and Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) schemes has been instrumental.
• Despite progress, regional disparities and challenges in reaching remote populations remain areas for continued focus.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NHM was launched in 2013 by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).
MMR is the number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births.
IMR is the number of infant deaths per 1,000 live births.
The target for MMR is 70 per 100,000 live births as per SDGs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key strategies adopted under the National Health Mission (NHM) that have led to improvements in maternal and child health outcomes.
Discuss the persistent challenges, such as regional disparities and infrastructure gaps, in achieving universal access to quality MCH services.
Evaluate the role of various government schemes like JSSK and RBSK in strengthening the public health infrastructure for MCH.
Suggest further policy interventions and programmatic approaches to accelerate progress towards India's SDG targets for health.
articles.imageDescription: A healthcare worker attending to a mother and child in a rural health center.
articles.trivia: India has achieved significant reduction in MMR, surpassing the 2015 Millennium Development Goal target.

articles.headline: New Species of Freshwater Fish Discovered in Western Ghats, Underscoring Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• Scientists have announced the discovery of a new species of freshwater fish in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats region of India.
• The discovery highlights the immense and often unexplored biodiversity of this UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• The new species, belonging to the genus 'Puntius', was found in a remote stream, emphasizing the need for focused ecological surveys.
• The finding underscores the critical importance of conservation efforts to protect the unique and fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats.
• Researchers called for stricter measures against habitat destruction, pollution, and climate change impacts in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It runs parallel to the western coast of the Indian peninsula.
Many endemic species are found here.
The new species belongs to the genus Puntius, a common group of freshwater fish.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the implications of discovering new species for conservation policy and management strategies in fragile ecosystems.
Examine the role of scientific research and community involvement in protecting and documenting biodiversity.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing developmental activities with biodiversity conservation in regions like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered, brightly colored freshwater fish.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is older than the Himalayas and influences the Indian monsoon weather pattern.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Stupa Remnants in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of significant remnants of an ancient Buddhist stupa in Odisha.
• The excavation site, yet to be fully identified, reveals structural remains and artifacts dating back to the early medieval period.
• This finding adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, which is known for sites like Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
• The discovery provides crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism and its architectural styles in Eastern India.
• Conservation and further exploration efforts are underway to preserve the site and understand its historical context.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Odisha has a significant Buddhist past, with several monastic complexes.
The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha refers to Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism in India, particularly in regional contexts like Odisha.
Analyze the architectural features and cultural importance of Buddhist stupas and monastic complexes in ancient India.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of heritage sites.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in protecting and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient brick structure, possibly a stupa, in a field.
articles.trivia: The Great Stupa at Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the oldest stone structures in India.

articles.headline: Nationwide Launch of Integrated E-Governance Portal for Land Records Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development launched a nationwide integrated e-governance portal for comprehensive land records management.
• The portal aims to digitize and integrate land records across states, enhancing transparency and reducing land-related disputes.
• Features include online access to land titles, mutation requests, cadastral maps, and property registration services.
• This initiative is a major step towards 'Digital India' and improving ease of doing business and living for citizens.
• It will leverage technologies like GIS and blockchain to ensure data security and integrity in land administration.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Land records are managed by state governments in India.
The Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) aims to modernize land records.
GIS (Geographic Information System) is used for mapping and analysis of geographical data.
Blockchain technology can enhance the security and immutability of land titles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges associated with traditional land records management in India and how e-governance initiatives can address them.
Analyze the potential benefits of an integrated land records portal for citizens, farmers, and economic development.
Examine the role of technology, such as GIS and blockchain, in enhancing transparency, efficiency, and security in land administration.
Evaluate the policy and implementation challenges in achieving complete digitization and integration of land records across diverse states.
articles.imageDescription: A digital interface showing land plots and maps on a computer screen, representing e-governance.
articles.trivia: Land disputes account for a significant portion of civil cases in India, often due to fragmented and outdated records.

articles.headline: India's Merchandise Exports Show Robust Growth in Q2 FY25 Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• India's merchandise exports registered robust growth in the second quarter of Fiscal Year 2024-25, defying global trade slowdowns.
• Key sectors contributing to this growth include engineering goods, electronics, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural products.
• The positive trend is attributed to government support measures, diversification of export markets, and increased competitiveness.
• Despite geopolitical tensions and demand fluctuations in major economies, Indian exporters demonstrated resilience.
• The Ministry of Commerce expressed optimism about achieving the annual export targets, emphasizing product and market diversification strategies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Q2 FY25 refers to the period from July to September 2024.
Merchandise exports refer to the export of physical goods.
Engineering goods and electronics are key drivers of India's exports.
The government aims to achieve a total export target of $1 trillion by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust merchandise export growth in the face of global economic uncertainties.
Discuss the importance of export diversification, both in terms of products and markets, for India's economic resilience.
Examine the government's role through various schemes and policies in boosting export competitiveness and addressing supply-side constraints.
Evaluate the challenges posed by global protectionism, geopolitical tensions, and currency fluctuations on India's export performance.
articles.imageDescription: Shipping containers stacked at a port, symbolizing global trade and exports.
articles.trivia: India's services exports have also shown remarkable growth in recent years, often outpacing merchandise exports.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Future Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Key achievements include the establishment of National Credit Framework (NCF) and the launch of various teacher training programs.
• However, challenges persist in infrastructure development, faculty recruitment, and ensuring equitable access across all regions.
• The review emphasized the need for continued stakeholder collaboration and increased investment to realize NEP's transformative vision.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
National Credit Framework (NCF) provides a common framework for earning, accumulating, and transferring credits.
Focus on 21st-century skills and holistic development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape.
Discuss the key successes and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP across different levels of education.
Examine how NEP addresses issues of access, equity, quality, and accountability in the Indian education system.
Evaluate the role of technology and vocational education integration in achieving the goals set forth by NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing educational advancement.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India since independence, following those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global AI Governance Framework at UN Summit
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for a global framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance at a high-level UN summit on digital cooperation.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for an inclusive, transparent, and equitable framework to address ethical, security, and developmental concerns of AI.
• India highlighted its 'AI for All' approach, focusing on responsible AI deployment for social good and economic growth.
• Concerns were raised about potential biases, data privacy, and the digital divide in the absence of robust global regulations.
• The push for a multilateral approach aims to prevent technological fragmentation and ensure AI benefits all nations.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India has its own National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence.
The UN General Assembly (UNGA) discusses global issues, including technology governance.
AI for All is India's vision for leveraging AI for inclusive growth.
Concerns about AI include job displacement, algorithmic bias, and autonomous weapons.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on global AI governance and its 'AI for All' approach in the context of international digital cooperation.
Analyze the ethical, economic, and security challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and the need for global regulation.
Examine the role of multilateral platforms like the UN in shaping international norms and standards for emerging technologies.
Evaluate the potential benefits and risks of AI for developing countries and how a global framework can ensure equitable access and benefits.

articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined in 1956 by John McCarthy.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes National Cybersecurity Strategy 2024 to Safeguard Digital Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced the finalization of India's National Cybersecurity Strategy 2024.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyber ecosystem, protecting critical information infrastructure and digital services.
• Key pillars include strengthening cyber defense capabilities, promoting public-private partnerships, and fostering a culture of cybersecurity awareness.
• It addresses emerging threats like ransomware, state-sponsored attacks, and data breaches, with a focus on indigenous solutions.
• The strategy emphasizes capacity building, skill development, and international collaboration to combat cybercrime effectively.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security forces and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cybersecurity incident response.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) includes sectors like energy, finance, transport, and government.
The strategy aims to make India a global hub for cybersecurity products and services.
Ransomware is a type of malicious software that encrypts data and demands payment for decryption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats faced by India and the rationale behind developing a comprehensive National Cybersecurity Strategy.
Analyze the key provisions and pillars of the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2024 and its potential impact on safeguarding India's digital space.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps, technological advancements, and international cooperation.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and citizen awareness in building a resilient cyber ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock superimposed on a digital circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India is among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New Strategic Railway Line Project in Northeast for Enhanced Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new strategic railway line project aimed at significantly enhancing connectivity in the North-Eastern region.
• The project involves the construction of a new broad-gauge line connecting key towns, facilitating passenger and freight movement.
• This initiative is part of the government's broader 'Act East Policy' and focus on infrastructure development in the strategically important region.
• It will boost economic activities, tourism, and provide better access to essential services for remote populations.
• Environmental impact assessments and land acquisition processes are already underway to ensure timely execution.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography.

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: The North-Eastern Region (NER) comprises eight states.
Broad gauge is a railway track gauge wider than standard gauge.
Act East Policy focuses on extensive diplomatic, economic, and cultural relations with Southeast Asian countries.
Railway connectivity is crucial for the landlocked states of the NER.
mainsPoints: Discuss the geographical and strategic significance of the North-Eastern Region (NER) for India's national security and economic integration.
Analyze how enhanced railway connectivity projects contribute to the 'Act East Policy' and regional development in the NER.
Examine the challenges associated with infrastructure development in the mountainous and ecologically sensitive NER.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of improved transportation networks on the lives of people in remote areas of the Northeast.
articles.imageDescription: A modern train traveling through a scenic mountainous landscape in Northeast India.
articles.trivia: The North-East Frontier Railway (NFR) is one of the 18 railway zones in India, primarily serving the Northeast states.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Multimodal Logistics Hub (NMLH) and Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), consider the following statements:
1. NMLH aims to integrate various modes of transport to reduce logistics costs.
2. EDFC connects the eastern and western coasts of India.
3. Both projects are components of the 'PM Gati Shakti' National Master Plan.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. NMLH aims to streamline supply chains and integrate different transport modes. Statement 2 is incorrect. The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) connects Punjab to West Bengal, primarily serving the eastern part of India, not both eastern and western coasts. Statement 3 is correct. Both NMLH and EDFC are crucial infrastructure projects under the 'PM Gati Shakti' National Master Plan.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, and related legal precedents is/are correct?
1. The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment (2017) recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
2. The DPDP Act, 2023, explicitly includes the 'right to be forgotten' and 'right to correction and erasure'.
3. 'Data Fiduciary' refers to the individual to whom the personal data relates.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Puttaswamy judgment established the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21. Statement 2 is correct. The DPDP Act, 2023, incorporates these rights for data principals. Statement 3 is incorrect. 'Data Fiduciary' is the entity determining the purpose and means of processing personal data, while the 'Data Principal' is the individual to whom the personal data relates.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC):
1. All SAARC member countries are also members of BIMSTEC.
2. BIMSTEC focuses on enhancing regional connectivity and trade in the Bay of Bengal region.
3. Myanmar and Thailand are among the founding members of BIMSTEC.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Not all SAARC members are BIMSTEC members (e.g., Pakistan, Afghanistan are not). Statement 2 is correct. BIMSTEC's primary objectives include promoting regional connectivity and trade. Statement 3 is incorrect. Myanmar and Thailand joined later. The founding members in 1997 were Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka, and Thailand (initially BIST-EC), with Myanmar joining in 1997 and Bhutan and Nepal in 2004.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Health Mission (NHM), which of the following is NOT a key objective or indicator of progress?
(a) Reducing Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR)
(b) Increasing Infant Mortality Rate (IMR)
(c) Enhancing institutional deliveries
(d) Improving immunization coverage
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Health Mission (NHM) aims to improve health outcomes, including reducing both Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) and Infant Mortality Rate (IMR). Therefore, 'increasing IMR' is explicitly NOT an objective; rather, reducing it is a core goal. Enhancing institutional deliveries and improving immunization coverage are key strategies to achieve these reductions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following bodies is responsible for constructing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas, as highlighted by the completion of the Nechiphu Tunnel?
(a) National Highways Authority of India (NHAI)
(b) Public Works Department (PWD)
(c) Border Roads Organisation (BRO)
(d) Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
options: (d) Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Defence, responsible for road construction and maintenance in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries. NHAI primarily manages national highways, PWDs manage state roads, and the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways is a central ministry for overall road policy.

mainsMocks.question: The inauguration of the National Multimodal Logistics Hub and key EDFC section underscores India's commitment to 'Viksit Bharat'. Discuss how integrated logistics infrastructure can drive economic growth, reduce costs, and enhance India's global competitiveness. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the significance of logistics for economic development. Explain how multimodal integration (road, rail, air, water) reduces costs and transit times. Link NMLH and EDFC to PM Gati Shakti. Discuss benefits for manufacturing, agriculture, exports, and job creation. Conclude on its role in achieving 'Viksit Bharat' and global competitiveness.

mainsMocks.question: The tragic incident in Hathras highlights the persistent challenge of superstitious practices in India. Critically analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to such beliefs and the need for a comprehensive national anti-superstition law. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism; GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the Hathras incident. Discuss factors like illiteracy, poverty, lack of scientific temperament, cultural inertia, and exploitation by charlatans. Argue for an anti-superstition law, citing examples from states with such laws. Discuss its challenges (e.g., defining 'superstition', religious freedom) and the importance of education and awareness alongside legal measures.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for a global AI governance framework at the UN summit reflects its vision for responsible AI. Examine the ethical, security, and developmental challenges posed by Artificial Intelligence and justify India's advocacy for an inclusive and transparent multilateral approach. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly state India's position. Enumerate challenges: ethical (bias, privacy), security (cyber warfare, autonomous weapons), developmental (digital divide, job displacement). Explain why a global, inclusive framework is needed to prevent fragmentation, ensure equitable access, and promote 'AI for All' for social good, rather than just economic or military advantage.

GS1.headline: Class 2 Student Killed in Hathras 'Tantric Sacrificial Rite'; Calls for Anti-Superstition Law Strengthen
GS1.explainer:
• A Class 2 student was tragically killed in Hathras district, Uttar Pradesh, reportedly as part of a 'tantric sacrificial rite'.
• The alleged perpetrators are the owners of the school where the child studied, who performed the act for the 'prosperity' of their institute.
• The incident has sparked outrage across the nation, bringing to fore the persistent issue of superstitious practices and black magic.
• Civil society organizations and legal experts are renewing calls for a comprehensive national anti-superstition law.
• Police have initiated an investigation and arrested the accused, promising swift justice in the heinous crime.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
prelimsPoints: Hathras district is in Uttar Pradesh.
The incident highlights the prevalence of superstitious beliefs in some parts of India.
Several states like Maharashtra and Karnataka have anti-superstition laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors that perpetuate superstitious beliefs and practices in modern India.
Analyze the need for a national anti-superstition law, considering constitutional provisions and fundamental rights.
Examine the role of education, awareness campaigns, and community engagement in eradicating social evils like human sacrifice.
Evaluate the challenges faced by law enforcement in tackling crimes motivated by superstitious beliefs.
GS1.imageDescription: A blurred image of a police investigation scene in a rural area, symbolizing law enforcement action.
GS1.trivia: Maharashtra was the first Indian state to enact an Anti-Superstition and Black Magic Act in 2013.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Stupa Remnants in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of significant remnants of an ancient Buddhist stupa in Odisha.
• The excavation site, yet to be fully identified, reveals structural remains and artifacts dating back to the early medieval period.
• This finding adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, which is known for sites like Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
• The discovery provides crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism and its architectural styles in Eastern India.
• Conservation and further exploration efforts are underway to preserve the site and understand its historical context.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Odisha has a significant Buddhist past, with several monastic complexes.
The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha refers to Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism in India, particularly in regional contexts like Odisha.
Analyze the architectural features and cultural importance of Buddhist stupas and monastic complexes in ancient India.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of heritage sites.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in protecting and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient brick structure, possibly a stupa, in a field.
GS1.trivia: The Great Stupa at Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the oldest stone structures in India.

GS2.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Summit: Leaders Vow to Bolster Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) Summit in New Delhi.
• Leaders from member states emphasized enhancing regional connectivity, trade, and economic cooperation.
• Key agreements were discussed on cross-border energy grids, digital connectivity, and multimodal transport corridors.
• The summit reiterated commitment to collective efforts in disaster management, climate change, and counter-terrorism.
• BIMSTEC aims to strengthen ties among countries in the Bay of Bengal region, bridging South and Southeast Asia.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Key Provisions in Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of significant provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment emphasized the state's legitimate interest in regulating data processing while safeguarding individual privacy.
• Petitions had challenged certain clauses regarding data fiduciaries' obligations and the process of grievance redressal.
• The Court affirmed the 'right to be forgotten' and 'right to correction and erasure' as integral aspects of data protection.
• The ruling provides clarity on the implementation of the new data protection regime, impacting tech companies and citizens alike.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to govern the processing of digital personal data.
The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment (2017) recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
Data Fiduciary refers to any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
Data Principal is the individual to whom the personal data relates.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act, 2023, between individual privacy rights and the state's legitimate interests in data processing.
Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital governance, e-commerce, and the tech industry in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in protecting citizens' data in an increasingly digital world, considering global best practices.
Examine the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, particularly concerning cross-border data flows and enforcement mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing judicial proceedings and law.
GS2.trivia: The DPDP Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, which was withdrawn.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Amendments to Election Laws for Enhanced Transparency in Campaign Finance
GS2.explainer:
• Parliament has successfully passed significant amendments to election laws, aiming to bring greater transparency to campaign finance.
• The amendments introduce stricter disclosure norms for political donations, including details of donors and beneficiaries.
• Provisions for digitizing election expenditure reports and real-time monitoring by the Election Commission of India (ECI) were also included.
• The move comes amidst long-standing demands for electoral reforms to curb the influence of black money in elections.
• Opposition parties, while supporting the intent, raised concerns about potential loopholes and the need for comprehensive reform.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: The Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs elections in India.
The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Electoral bonds were introduced to promote transparency in political funding, though their anonymity aspect was controversial.
Article 324 of the Constitution deals with the Election Commission.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of electoral finance regulations in India and the persistent challenges in ensuring transparency.
Discuss the significance of the recent amendments to election laws in strengthening democratic processes and curbing corruption.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India in advocating for and implementing electoral reforms.
Examine the debate around political funding mechanisms, including electoral bonds, and suggest further reforms for greater accountability.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with a hand inserting a vote, symbolizing electoral process.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in India were held in 1951-52.

GS2.headline: India and France Ink MoU for Joint Research in Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence
GS2.explainer:
• India and France signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for enhanced cooperation in cutting-edge technologies like Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence (AI).
• The agreement aims to foster joint research projects, talent exchange, and development of shared technological platforms.
• This collaboration is part of the strategic partnership between the two nations, focusing on emerging and critical technologies.
• It will facilitate the exchange of scientific expertise and resources to accelerate innovation in both countries.
• The partnership seeks to leverage the strengths of both nations in building future-ready technological capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
